title: Failed to build verilator with some DPI function on windows (msys2)
body:
I tried to build a verilator model on msys2 (windows). This model has 2 modules using DPI.
a) a JTAG interface, which calls C-functions from SystemVerilog
b) a Memload function, which allows to load the memory from C

The compilation works on CentOS and Debian. On Msys2 it works with the JTAG interface but it 
does not compile with the memload function. 

I get the error message: 
g++    digital_top.o jtagdpi.o spi_slave.o tb.o verilated.o verilated_dpi.o verilated_vcd_c.o Vdimad01_digital_top__ALL.a    -o Vdimad01_digital_top -lm -lstdc++
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-msys/9.3.0/../../../../x86_64-pc-msys/bin/ld: digital_top.o:digital_top.cpp:(.text+0x182): undefined reference to `WriteVmemToMem'
digital_top.o:digital_top.cpp:(.text+0x182): relocation truncated to fit: R_X86_64_PC32 against undefined symbol `WriteVmemToMem'
collect2: Fehler: ld gab 1 als Ende-Status zurück

WriteVmemToMem is the C-function which triggers the SystemVerilog function. See attached code.

Any idea, why this is a problem (only) on windows?

P.S.: I used msys2 and not mingw, as this is not supported by the JTAG DPI function, which uses a linux style network implementation
[memload.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/4466334/memload.zip)
[sp_ram.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/4466337/sp_ram.zip)
